message:
test(ffs-journal): use sync_counter for true fsync count assertion

The fsync_count_is_per_epoch_not_per_transaction test was asserting
that coord.notifier().durable_epoch() equals max_epoch, then naming
the result "sync_count". That's incidentally true (durable_epoch
moves once per group commit by design) but it doesn't actually
prove the underlying contract — which is that the *writer* observed
exactly one fsync call per epoch flushed, not one per submitted
transaction.

If the GroupCommitCoordinator ever changed to emit redundant
fsyncs (or skip them), the old assertion would still pass because
durable_epoch is updated from the coordinator's own bookkeeping,
not from the syscall count.

Switched to TrackingWriter::sync_counter() (already exposed by the
test harness) which is the AtomicU64 counter incremented exactly
once per writer.sync() call. The test now asserts both:

  - sync_counter.load(Ordering::Acquire) == max_epoch (the new
    observation: one fsync per epoch from the writer's view), and
  - coord.notifier().durable_epoch() == max_epoch (the existing
    invariant on the coordinator side, retained as a sanity check).

The u64::try_from()/Ok() pattern handles the AtomicU64 -> usize
widening cleanly without unwrap. No production code changes; this
is a stricter assertion that catches a class of regression the
previous form silently allowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
